summary: sea-bound-siren postinstall script exfiltrates host data and executes remote commands
details: The package's postinstall.js executes automatically on `npm install` and combines several attack primitives: it imports `fs`, `http`, `os`, and `child_process`; reads local files via `fs.readFileSync`; collects host identifiers including `os.hostname()` and `process.cwd()`; issues HTTP GET/`http.request` calls to a hardcoded endpoint (http://127.0.0.1 visible in the evidence, indicating a callback channel); and shells out via `curl` from child_process at multiple lines. The combination of system-information collection, filesystem reads, outbound HTTP from a lifecycle hook, and curl-based command execution is the canonical install-time exfiltration / command-and-control shape. Installing this package causes the installer's machine to leak host metadata and run attacker-influenced network commands without user consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
